title: tragacantha andalanica
intro: Tragacantha andalanica, also known as Andalucian sarsaparilla or Spanish sarsaparilla, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a perennial plant with a woody base, from which it produces a thick, hairy stem, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in treating rheumatism, skin conditions, and digestive issues due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Tragacantha andalanica is often used in teas, infusions, or as a dried root extract to harness its medicinal properties.
